heading: Designation of the data protection officer
text: 1. The controller and the processor shall designate a data protection officer in any case where:
screen_justification: This paragraph establishes a substantive obligation for controllers and processors to designate a data protection officer.

next_sibling.text: (a) the processing is carried out by a public authority or body, except for courts acting in their judicial capacity;

heading: Designation of the data protection officer
text: 2. A group of undertakings may appoint a single data protection officer provided that a data protection officer is easily accessible from each establishment.
screen_justification: This paragraph grants a permission to a group of undertakings, subject to a specific condition, thus containing a substantive deontic norm.

previous_sibling.text: (c) the core activities of the controller or the processor consist of processing on a large scale of special categories of data pursuant to Article 9 and personal data relating to criminal convictions and offences referred to in Article 10.

next_sibling.text: 3. Where the controller or the processor is a public authority or body, a single data protection officer may be designated for several such authorities or bodies, taking account of their organisational structure and size.

heading: Designation of the data protection officer
text: 3. Where the controller or the processor is a public authority or body, a single data protection officer may be designated for several such authorities or bodies, taking account of their organisational structure and size.
screen_justification: This paragraph provides a substantive permission for public authorities regarding the designation of a data protection officer.

previous_sibling.text: 2. A group of undertakings may appoint a single data protection officer provided that a data protection officer is easily accessible from each establishment.

next_sibling.text: 4. In cases other than those referred to in paragraph 1, the controller or processor or associations and other bodies representing categories of controllers or processors may or, where required by Union or Member State law shall, designate a data protection officer. The data protection officer may act for such associations and other bodies representing controllers or processors.

heading: Designation of the data protection officer
text: 4. In cases other than those referred to in paragraph 1, the controller or processor or associations and other bodies representing categories of controllers or processors may or, where required by Union or Member State law shall, designate a data protection officer. The data protection officer may act for such associations and other bodies representing controllers or processors.
screen_justification: This paragraph establishes a conditional obligation or permission for controllers and processors to designate a data protection officer.

previous_sibling.text: 3. Where the controller or the processor is a public authority or body, a single data protection officer may be designated for several such authorities or bodies, taking account of their organisational structure and size.

next_sibling.text: 5. The data protection officer shall be designated on the basis of professional qualities and, in particular, expert knowledge of data protection law and practices and the ability to fulfil the tasks referred to in Article 39.

cross_references.text: 1. The controller and the processor shall designate a data protection officer in any case where:

heading: Designation of the data protection officer
text: 5. The data protection officer shall be designated on the basis of professional qualities and, in particular, expert knowledge of data protection law and practices and the ability to fulfil the tasks referred to in Article 39.
screen_justification: This paragraph specifies the professional qualities required for designating a data protection officer, constituting a substantive norm.

previous_sibling.text: 4. In cases other than those referred to in paragraph 1, the controller or processor or associations and other bodies representing categories of controllers or processors may or, where required by Union or Member State law shall, designate a data protection officer. The data protection officer may act for such associations and other bodies representing controllers or processors.

next_sibling.text: 6. The data protection officer may be a staff member of the controller or processor, or fulfil the tasks on the basis of a service contract.

cross_references.text: 1. The data protection officer shall have at least the following tasks:

heading: Designation of the data protection officer
text: 6. The data protection officer may be a staff member of the controller or processor, or fulfil the tasks on the basis of a service contract.
screen_justification: This paragraph specifies a permission regarding the employment status of the data protection officer for controllers or processors.

previous_sibling.text: 5. The data protection officer shall be designated on the basis of professional qualities and, in particular, expert knowledge of data protection law and practices and the ability to fulfil the tasks referred to in Article 39.

next_sibling.text: 7. The controller or the processor shall publish the contact details of the data protection officer and communicate them to the supervisory authority.

heading: Designation of the data protection officer
text: 7. The controller or the processor shall publish the contact details of the data protection officer and communicate them to the supervisory authority.
screen_justification: This paragraph contains a substantive deontic norm, obliging controllers and processors to publish and communicate DPO contact details.

previous_sibling.text: 6. The data protection officer may be a staff member of the controller or processor, or fulfil the tasks on the basis of a service contract.

heading: Position of the data protection officer
text: 1. The controller and the processor shall ensure that the data protection officer is involved, properly and in a timely manner, in all issues which relate to the protection of personal data.
screen_justification: This paragraph establishes a substantive obligation for controllers and processors regarding the involvement of the data protection officer.

next_sibling.text: 2. The controller and processor shall support the data protection officer in performing the tasks referred to in Article 39 by providing resources necessary to carry out those tasks and access to personal data and processing operations, and to maintain his or her expert knowledge.

heading: Position of the data protection officer
text: 2. The controller and processor shall support the data protection officer in performing the tasks referred to in Article 39 by providing resources necessary to carry out those tasks and access to personal data and processing operations, and to maintain his or her expert knowledge.
screen_justification: This paragraph imposes a substantive obligation on controllers and processors to support the data protection officer.

previous_sibling.text: 1. The controller and the processor shall ensure that the data protection officer is involved, properly and in a timely manner, in all issues which relate to the protection of personal data.

next_sibling.text: 3. The controller and processor shall ensure that the data protection officer does not receive any instructions regarding the exercise of those tasks. He or she shall not be dismissed or penalised by the controller or the processor for performing his tasks. The data protection officer shall directly report to the highest management level of the controller or the processor.

cross_references.text: 1. The data protection officer shall have at least the following tasks:

heading: Position of the data protection officer
text: 3. The controller and processor shall ensure that the data protection officer does not receive any instructions regarding the exercise of those tasks. He or she shall not be dismissed or penalised by the controller or the processor for performing his tasks. The data protection officer shall directly report to the highest management level of the controller or the processor.
screen_justification: This paragraph contains substantive deontic norms imposing obligations on controllers and processors regarding the DPO's independence and reporting.

previous_sibling.text: 2. The controller and processor shall support the data protection officer in performing the tasks referred to in Article 39 by providing resources necessary to carry out those tasks and access to personal data and processing operations, and to maintain his or her expert knowledge.

next_sibling.text: 4. Data subjects may contact the data protection officer with regard to all issues related to processing of their personal data and to the exercise of their rights under this Regulation.

heading: Position of the data protection officer
text: 4. Data subjects may contact the data protection officer with regard to all issues related to processing of their personal data and to the exercise of their rights under this Regulation.
screen_justification: This paragraph grants a permission to data subjects, constituting a substantive deontic norm.

previous_sibling.text: 3. The controller and processor shall ensure that the data protection officer does not receive any instructions regarding the exercise of those tasks. He or she shall not be dismissed or penalised by the controller or the processor for performing his tasks. The data protection officer shall directly report to the highest management level of the controller or the processor.

next_sibling.text: 5. The data protection officer shall be bound by secrecy or confidentiality concerning the performance of his or her tasks, in accordance with Union or Member State law.

heading: Position of the data protection officer
text: 5. The data protection officer shall be bound by secrecy or confidentiality concerning the performance of his or her tasks, in accordance with Union or Member State law.
screen_justification: This paragraph establishes a substantive obligation for the data protection officer regarding secrecy and confidentiality.

previous_sibling.text: 4. Data subjects may contact the data protection officer with regard to all issues related to processing of their personal data and to the exercise of their rights under this Regulation.

next_sibling.text: 6. The data protection officer may fulfil other tasks and duties. The controller or processor shall ensure that any such tasks and duties do not result in a conflict of interests.

heading: Tasks of the data protection officer
text: 2. The data protection officer shall in the performance of his or her tasks have due regard to the risk associated with processing operations, taking into account the nature, scope, context and purposes of processing.
screen_justification: This paragraph imposes a substantive obligation on the data protection officer regarding risk assessment in their tasks.

previous_sibling.text: (e) to act as the contact point for the supervisory authority on issues relating to processing, including the prior consultation referred to in Article 36, and to consult, where appropriate, with regard to any other matter.
